agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics architecture using region-sovereign data planes for EU, US, and APAC with a lightweight global control plane for cross-region observability. Each region owns its customer and courier data end-to-end, enforcing data residency at the infrastructure level. A global metrics aggregation layer (not raw data) feeds the operations dashboard. Regional failover uses active-passive within each region's availability zones rather than cross-region failover, preserving data residency invariants even during outages.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Regional Data Plane (EU / US / APAC)","responsibility":"Hosts all customer-facing services, courier assignment, order management, and regional databases. Each instance is a fully self-contained deployment of the core logistics platform. Owns all PII and operational data for its region.","boundaries":"Hard boundary at region edge. No customer PII crosses this boundary. All writes and reads for regional customers are served locally. Each data plane has its own database cluster, cache layer, message broker, and compute."},{"name":"Regional API Gateway","responsibility":"Entry point for all customer and courier traffic in a region. Enforces authentication, rate limiting, and routes requests to regional microservices. Geo-DNS directs users to their home region's gateway.","boundaries":"Accepts only traffic routed via Geo-DNS or explicit region pinning. Rejects requests that don't match the region's customer registry."},{"name":"Regional Courier Assignment Service","responsibility":"Matches available couriers to deliveries within the region. Enforces the invariant that couriers can only be assigned to deliveries in their operating region.","boundaries":"Reads only from the regional courier registry and regional order queue. No cross-region courier data is accessible."},{"name":"Regional Event Bus","responsibility":"Async event backbone within a region. Publishes domain events (order created, delivery completed, courier assigned) for intra-region consumers. Also publishes sanitized, aggregated metric events to the Global Metrics Collector.","boundaries":"Raw events with PII stay within the region. Only pre-aggregated, anonymized metric payloads are emitted to the global tier."},{"name":"Global Control Plane","responsibility":"Lightweight coordination layer. Manages region registry, global configuration (feature flags, routing rules), and the global operations dashboard data pipeline. Does NOT store or process customer PII.","boundaries":"Receives only aggregated metrics (counts, latencies, throughput, error rates). Never receives customer names, addresses, or courier PII. Deployed in a neutral region or multi-region with no PII sensitivity."},{"name":"Global Metrics Aggregator","responsibility":"Collects pre-aggregated metric streams from each regional event bus. Stores time-series data for the global operations dashboard. Handles regional availability detection.","boundaries":"Ingests only anonymized operational metrics. If a region stops sending metrics, the aggregator marks that region as degraded in the dashboard."},{"name":"Global Operations Dashboard","responsibility":"Real-time view of all-region operational health: delivery volumes, SLA compliance, courier utilization, error rates. Consumed by global ops team.","boundaries":"Read-only view over the Global Metrics Aggregator. Cannot drill into individual customer or courier records (those require region-specific admin access)."},{"name":"Regional Degraded-Mode Service","responsibility":"Serves cached, read-only responses when the primary regional services are unhealthy. Allows customers to view recent order status and estimated delivery times from a read replica or local cache.","boundaries":"Activated only during regional primary failure. Serves stale-but-safe data from within the same region. No writes accepted in degraded mode except queuing retry requests."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Geo-DNS","to":"Regional API Gateway","contract":"DNS resolution returns the closest healthy regional gateway IP. Health checks every 10s. Failover within-region to secondary AZ, never cross-region for data-bearing traffic.","coupling_type":"infrastructure"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Regional Courier Assignment Service","contract":"gRPC/REST: POST /assignments {orderId, regionId} → {assignmentId, courierId}. Region ID is implicit from the gateway's deployment context.","coupling_type":"runtime-sync"},{"from":"Regional Event Bus","to":"Global Metrics Aggregator","contract":"Outbound metric events: {regionId, metricType, value, timestamp, window}. Schema is a Published Language. No PII fields. Push-based with at-least-once delivery.","coupling_type":"async-data"},{"from":"Global Metrics Aggregator","to":"Global Operations Dashboard","contract":"WebSocket/SSE subscription for real-time metric streams. REST for historical queries. All data is pre-aggregated by region.","coupling_type":"runtime-sync"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Regional Degraded-Mode Service","contract":"Circuit breaker pattern: if primary services fail health checks for >5s, gateway routes read requests to degraded-mode service. Write requests return 503 with retry-after header.","coupling_type":"runtime-sync-fallback"},{"from":"Global Control Plane","to":"Regional Data Plane (EU / US / APAC)","contract":"Configuration sync: feature flags, routing rules, schema versions pushed via config replication. Regional planes pull on startup and subscribe to change events. No PII in config payloads.","coupling_type":"async-config"}],"data_flow":"Customer request → Geo-DNS resolves to home region → Regional API Gateway authenticates and routes → Regional services process (order, assignment, tracking) using regional DB → Domain events published to Regional Event Bus → Regional consumers update read models, trigger notifications → Metric events (anonymized, aggregated) emitted from Regional Event Bus to Global Metrics Aggregator → Dashboard renders real-time cross-region view. On regional failure: gateway circuit breaker activates → degraded-mode service responds from cache/read-replica → metric stream to global aggregator stops → dashboard shows region as degraded.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Data Sovereignty via Regional Isolation","rationale":"GDPR and CCPA compliance require PII to never leave its jurisdiction. Rather than encrypting-at-rest with regional keys (which still risks transit violations), we enforce sovereignty architecturally: each region is a complete, independent deployment. This makes compliance verifiable by infrastructure topology, not just policy."},{"pattern":"Strangler Fig for Migration","rationale":"Existing EU-only platform can be progressively decomposed. New regions deploy the same artifact stack. EU remains primary until US and APAC are validated. Shared global components are extracted incrementally."},{"pattern":"CQRS for Global Dashboard","rationale":"The global dashboard is a read-only projection of regional write models. Separating the read path (aggregated metrics) from write path (regional operations) allows the dashboard to function independently of any single region's health."},{"pattern":"Circuit Breaker with Graceful Degradation","rationale":"Regional failures should not cascade. Circuit breaker at the API gateway level detects backend failures and routes to a degraded-mode service that serves cached data, meeting the requirement for degraded-but-functional service."},{"pattern":"Event-Carried State Transfer (Metrics Only)","rationale":"Regional event buses publish anonymized metric summaries to the global tier. This avoids synchronous cross-region calls (latency, coupling) and ensures PII never transits to the global layer."},{"pattern":"Geo-DNS with Health Checks","rationale":"Sub-100ms latency requires serving customers from infrastructure physically close to them. Geo-DNS routes to the nearest healthy region. No cross-region hops for customer-facing traffic."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order","OrderLine","DeliveryWindow"]},{"name":"Courier Operations","classification":"core","aggregates":["Courier","CourierAvailability","Assignment"]},{"name":"Customer Identity","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Customer","CustomerAddress","DataResidencyRecord"]},{"name":"Delivery Tracking","classification":"core","aggregates":["Delivery","TrackingEvent","ProofOfDelivery"]},{"name":"Global Operations Intelligence","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["RegionHealthMetric","AggregatedKPI","AlertRule"]},{"name":"Billing & Invoicing","classification":"generic","aggregates":["Invoice","PaymentRecord"]},{"name":"Region Configuration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["RegionDefinition","FeatureFlag","ComplianceRule"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Courier Operations","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order Management publishes OrderCreated events; Courier Operations consumes them to trigger assignment. Order Management dictates the delivery requirements contract."},{"upstream":"Courier Operations","downstream":"Delivery Tracking","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Assignment decisions flow downstream as AssignmentConfirmed events that Delivery Tracking consumes to initialize tracking."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Global Operations Intelligence","relationship":"Published Language. Order Management emits anonymized metric events conforming to a shared metrics schema. Global Ops consumes without influencing the upstream model."},{"upstream":"Courier Operations","downstream":"Global Operations Intelligence","relationship":"Published Language. Same pattern — anonymized courier utilization metrics flow to global aggregation."},{"upstream":"Region Configuration","downstream":"All Regional Contexts","relationship":"Conformist. Regional services conform to the configuration schema defined by the global control plane. Minimal coupling — only feature flags and routing rules."},{"upstream":"Customer Identity","downstream":"Order Management","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer. Order Management maintains its own lightweight customer reference (customerId + region) and does not import the full identity model. ACL translates at the boundary."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Fully independent regional deployments (no shared database, no cross-region replication of PII)","gains":"Iron-clad data residency compliance. Each region can be audited independently. No risk of accidental PII leakage via replication lag or misconfigured sync. Regulatory posture is provable by architecture, not policy.","sacrifices":"Higher infrastructure cost (3x the database, compute, and operational tooling). No global customer view — a customer who moves regions requires explicit data portability process. Deployment complexity increases (must deploy to 3 regions).","alternatives_considered":["Single global database with row-level security and regional encryption keys — rejected because transit of encrypted PII across regions may still violate GDPR data residency requirements and is harder to audit","Cross-region replication with PII filtering — rejected because filter misconfiguration is a compliance catastrophe"]},{"decision":"Aggregated metrics only in the global tier (no raw events)","gains":"Eliminates any PII exposure risk in the global layer. Simpler compliance story. Global tier can be hosted anywhere.","sacrifices":"Global ops team cannot drill into individual orders or customers from the dashboard — must use region-specific admin tools. Troubleshooting cross-region issues requires switching between regional admin interfaces.","alternatives_considered":["Tokenized PII in global tier — adds complexity and still creates a linkability risk","Full event replication with PII redaction — redaction errors are a compliance risk"]},{"decision":"Degraded mode via regional cache/read-replica rather than cross-region failover","gains":"Preserves data residency during outages. No risk of EU customer traffic being served from US infrastructure. Simpler failover logic.","sacrifices":"Degraded mode is truly degraded — no writes, stale data. Customers cannot place new orders during a regional outage. Recovery depends on regional infrastructure restoration.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region active-active with conflict resolution — violates data residency requirements","Multi-region active-active within the same jurisdiction (e.g., EU-West + EU-Central) — viable and recommended as a phase 2 enhancement, but adds complexity"]},{"decision":"Courier region-locking enforced at the domain model level (not just routing)","gains":"The invariant that a courier cannot be assigned cross-region is enforced in the Courier aggregate itself, not just at the API layer. Defense in depth.","sacrifices":"Couriers operating near borders (e.g., EU/APAC boundary) cannot be dynamically reassigned. Requires explicit region transfer process.","alternatives_considered":["Soft region preference with override capability — rejected because it weakens the invariant and creates compliance edge cases"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions","evolution_path":"The architecture is a template: deploy the same artifact stack to a new region, register it in the Global Control Plane, configure Geo-DNS. The regional data plane is self-contained by design, making region addition a deployment exercise rather than an architectural change."},{"area":"Intra-jurisdiction multi-AZ active-active","evolution_path":"Phase 2 can add active-active within a jurisdiction (e.g., EU-West-1 + EU-Central-1) for true HA without violating data residency. The regional event bus already supports this — just add a second consumer group in the secondary AZ."},{"area":"Global product catalog or pricing","evolution_path":"Non-PII data like product catalogs, pricing rules, and service definitions can be replicated globally via the Global Control Plane without compliance risk. This is a natural extension of the config sync interface."},{"area":"Regional microservice evolution","evolution_path":"Each region's internal architecture can evolve independently. One region could adopt event sourcing while another stays with traditional CRUD, as long as the external contracts (API, metric events) remain stable."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Data residency boundary","reason":"This is the core compliance invariant. PII must never cross regional boundaries. This constraint is non-negotiable and should be enforced at infrastructure (network policy), platform (no cross-region DB access), and application (domain model) levels. Changing this requires legal review."},{"area":"Metric event schema (Published Language)","reason":"The contract between regional event buses and the Global Metrics Aggregator must be stable. Breaking changes require coordinated rollout across all regions. Version the schema from day one."},{"area":"Region assignment on customer and courier entities","reason":"Once a customer or courier is assigned to a region, their data lives there. Migration requires an explicit, auditable data portability process. This rigidity is intentional — it prevents accidental data residency violations."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Clock skew and metric ordering across regions creates inconsistent global dashboard views","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use logical timestamps (vector clocks or Lamport timestamps) in metric events. Dashboard should display per-region timestamps and tolerate ordering gaps. Do not attempt global total ordering — it's unnecessary for operational metrics."},{"risk":"Regional deployment drift — regions running different versions causes behavioral inconsistency","severity":"high","mitigation":"Enforce same-version deployment across regions using a global CI/CD pipeline with canary releases. Region Configuration context tracks deployed versions. Automated drift detection alerts."},{"risk":"Degraded mode cache becomes stale beyond usefulness during extended outage","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Set explicit TTLs on cached data (e.g., 30 min for order status, 5 min for courier location). Display staleness indicators in the UI. After TTL expiration, show 'service unavailable' rather than misleading stale data."},{"risk":"Latency budget blown by regional service chain depth","severity":"high","mitigation":"Target <100ms requires careful service decomposition within each region. Limit synchronous call chains to 2-3 hops max. Use async processing for non-latency-critical paths. Mandate latency budgets per service (e.g., gateway: 5ms, auth: 10ms, core logic: 50ms, DB: 30ms, margin: 5ms)."},{"risk":"Compliance violation via logging or observability pipeline accidentally shipping PII to global tier","severity":"critical","mitigation":"All log and metric pipelines from regional to global must pass through a PII scrubbing gateway. Automated PII detection scanning on global-tier storage. Regular compliance audits. Infrastructure-level network policies blocking raw log export from regional VPCs."},{"risk":"Geo-DNS misconfiguration routes customers to wrong region","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Defense in depth: Regional API Gateway validates that the authenticated customer's home region matches the gateway's region. If mismatch, return a redirect to the correct region with no data processing. Automated Geo-DNS configuration tests in CI."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout: (1) Extract the existing EU monolith into the regional data plane template — define clear service boundaries, event contracts, and metric emission points. (2) Deploy the Global Control Plane and Metrics Aggregator alongside the existing EU deployment. (3) Deploy US region using the same template, validate with synthetic traffic. (4) Deploy APAC. (5) Enable Geo-DNS routing. (6) Harden degraded-mode and circuit breaker behavior. Each phase should be independently shippable and reversible.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/terraform/modules/regional-data-plane/ — Terraform module for region deployment (VPC, DB, compute, event bus)","infra/terraform/modules/global-control-plane/ — Global metrics aggregator, config store, dashboard infra","services/api-gateway/middleware/region-validator.ts — Validates customer region matches gateway region","services/courier-assignment/domain/courier-aggregate.ts — Enforces region-lock invariant on Courier aggregate","services/shared/events/metric-event-schema.ts — Published Language schema for cross-region metrics","services/api-gateway/middleware/circuit-breaker.ts — Circuit breaker with fallback to degraded-mode service","services/degraded-mode/cache-service.ts — Serves cached read-only responses during outages","infra/geo-dns/health-check-config.yaml — Geo-DNS routing and health check configuration","docs/adr/001-multi-region-data-sovereignty.md — Architecture Decision Record for data residency approach","docs/adr/002-global-metrics-aggregation.md — ADR for metrics-only global tier decision"],"patterns_to_follow":"Each regional service should be a self-contained bounded context deployment. Use domain events for intra-region communication. Use anonymized metric events for cross-region communication. Enforce data residency at three levels: infrastructure (network policies), platform (no cross-region DB connections), application (domain model invariants). Circuit breaker at gateway level. Published Language for all cross-region contracts.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do not introduce a global user directory or customer lookup service — this violates data residency","Do not use cross-region database replication for PII tables, even with encryption","Do not allow the global operations dashboard to query regional databases directly","Do not assume Geo-DNS alone enforces region correctness — validate at the application layer","Do not share message broker clusters across regions — each region must have its own broker","Do not log PII in metric events — scrub at the source, not at the destination","Do not attempt cross-region distributed transactions — accept eventual consistency for global views","Do not skip the degraded-mode implementation — 'EU stays functional during outage' is a hard requirement"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":82,"high_confidence_areas":["Data residency enforcement via regional isolation is the correct approach for GDPR/CCPA","Aggregated metrics for global dashboard avoids PII leakage","Circuit breaker + degraded mode for regional resilience","Courier region-locking at the domain level"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact latency budget allocation across the regional service chain depends on current service decomposition and database performance which are unknown","Whether the existing EU platform is a monolith or already microservices affects the migration strategy significantly","APAC data residency requirements are not specified — assumed similar to EU/US but actual regulations vary by country"],"assumptions":["The existing platform is deployable as a set of containerized services or can be refactored into one","Each region will have at least 2 availability zones for intra-region HA","The global operations team has separate admin access to each region for detailed troubleshooting","Customer region assignment is determined at registration time and rarely changes","Budget supports 3x infrastructure cost for full regional independence"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics architecture using region-sovereign data planes for EU, US, and APAC with a lightweight global control plane for cross-region observability. Each region owns its customer and courier data end-to-end, enforcing data residency at the infrastructure level. A global metrics aggregation layer (not raw data) feeds the operations dashboard. Regional failover uses active-passive within each region's availability zones rather than cross-region failover, preser

Enforces the invariant that couriers can only be assigned to deliveries in their operating region.","boundaries":"Reads only from the regional courier registry and regional order queue. REST for historical queries. All data is pre-aggregated by region.","coupling_type":"runtime-sync"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Regional Degraded-Mode Service","contract":"Circuit breaker pattern: if primary services fail health checks for >5s, gateway routes read requests to degraded-mode service. Breaking changes require coordinated rollout across all regions. Version the schema from day one."},{"area":"Region assignment on customer and courier entities","reason":"Once a customer or courier is assigned to a region, their data lives there. Migration requires an explicit, auditable data portability process. This rigidity is intentional — it prevents accidental data residency violations."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Clock skew and metric ordering across regions creates inconsistent global dashboard views","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use logical timestamps (vector clocks or Lamport timestamps) in metric events. Dashboard should display per-region timestamps and tolerate ordering gaps. Do not attempt global total ordering — it's unnecessary for operational metrics."},{"risk":"Regional deployment drift — regions running different versions causes behavioral inconsistency","severity":"high","mitigation":"Enforce same-version deployment across regions using a global CI/CD pipeline with canary releases. Region Configuration context tracks deployed versions. Automated drift detection alerts."},{"risk":"Degraded mode cache becomes stale beyond usefulness during extended outage","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Set explicit TTLs on cached data (e.g., 30 min for order status, 5 min for courier location). Display staleness indicators in the UI. After TTL expiration, show 'service unavailable' rather than misleading stale data."},{"risk":"Latency budget blown by regional service chain depth","severity":"high","mitigation":"Target <100ms requires careful service decomposition within each region. Limit synchronous call chains to 2-3 hops max. Use async processing for non-latency-critical paths. Mandate latency budgets per service (e.g., gateway: 5ms, auth: 10ms, core logic: 50ms, DB: 30ms, margin: 5ms)."},{"risk":"Compliance violation via logging or observability pipeline accidentally shipping PII to global tier","severity":"critical","mitigation":"All log and metric pipelines from regional to global must pass through a PII scrubbing gateway. Automated PII detection scanning on global-tier storage. Regular compliance audits. Infrastructure-level network policies blocking raw log export from regional VPCs."},{"risk":"Geo-DNS misconfiguration routes customers to wrong region","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Defense in depth: Regional API Gateway validates that the authenticated customer's home region matches the gateway's region. If mismatch, return a redirect to the correct region with no data processing. Automated Geo-DNS configuration tests in CI."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout: (1) Extract the existing EU monolith into the regional data plane template — define clear service boundaries, event contracts, and metric emission points. (2) Deploy the Global Control Plane and Metrics Aggregator alongside the existing EU deployment. (3) Deploy US region using the same template, validate with synthetic traffic. (4) Deploy APAC. (5) Enable Geo-DNS routing. (6) Harden degraded-mode and circuit breaker behavior. Each phase should be independently shippable and reversible.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/terraform/modules/regional-data-plane/ — Terraform module for region deployment (VPC, DB, compute, event bus)","infra/terraform/modules/global-control-plane/ — Global metrics aggregator, config store, dashboard infra","services/api-gateway/middleware/region-validator.ts — Validates customer region matches gateway region","services/courier-assignment/domain/courier-aggregate.ts — Enforces region-lock invariant on Courier aggregate","services/shared/events/metric-event-schema.ts — Published Language schema for cross-region metrics","services/api-gateway/middleware/circuit-breaker.ts — Circuit breaker with fallback to degraded-mode service","services/degraded-mode/cache-service.ts — Serves cached read-only responses during outages","infra/geo-dns/health-check-config.yaml — Geo-DNS routing and health check configuration","docs/adr/001-multi-region-data-sovereignty.md — Architecture Decision Record for data residency approach","docs/adr/002-global-metrics-aggregation.md — ADR for metrics-only global tier decision"],"patterns_to_follow":"Each regional service should be a self-contained bounded context deployment. Use domain events for intra-region communication. Use anonymized metric events for cross-region communication. Enforce data residency at three levels: infrastructure (network policies), platform (no cross-region DB connections), application (domain model invariants). Circuit breaker at gateway level. Published Language for all cross-region contracts.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do not introduce a global user directory or customer lookup service — this violates data residency","Do not use cross-region database replication for PII tables, even with encryption","Do not allow the global operations dashboard to query regional databases directly","Do not assume Geo-DNS alone enforces region correctness — validate at the application layer","Do not share message broker clusters across regions — each region must have its own broker","Do not log PII in metric events — scrub at the source, not at the destination","Do not attempt cross-region distributed transactions — accept eventual consistency for global views","Do not skip the degraded-mode implementation — 'EU stays functional during outage' is a hard requirement"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":82,"high_confidence_areas":["Data residency enforcement via regional isolation is the correct approach for GDPR/CCPA","Aggregated metrics for global dashboard avoids PII leakage","Circuit breaker + degraded mode for regional resilience","Courier region-locking at the domain level"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact latency budget allocation across the regional service chain depends on current service decomposition and database performance which are unknown","Whether the existing EU platform is a monolith or already microservices affects the migration strategy significantly","APAC data residency requirements are not specified — assumed similar to EU/US but actual regulations vary by country"],"assumptions":["The existing platform is deployable as a set of containerized services or can be refactored into one","Each region will have at least 2 availability zones for intra-region HA","The global operations team has separate admin access to each region for detailed troubleshooting","Customer region assignment is determined at registration time and rarely changes","Budget supports 3x infrastructure cost for full regional independence"]}}

trace.result: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics architecture using region-sovereign data planes for EU, US, and APAC with a lightweight global control plane for cross-region observability. Each region owns its customer and courier data end-to-end, enforcing data residency at the infrastructure level. A global metrics aggregation layer (not raw data) feeds the operations dashboard. Regional failover uses active-passive within each region's availability zones rather than cross-region failover, preserving data residency invariants even during outages.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Regional Data Plane (EU / US / APAC)","responsibility":"Hosts all customer-facing services, courier assignment, order management, and regional databases. Each instance is a fully self-contained deployment of the core logistics platform. Owns all PII and operational data for its region.","boundaries":"Hard boundary at region edge. No customer PII crosses this boundary. All writes and reads for regional customers are served locally. Each data plane has its own database cluster, cache layer, message broker, and compute."},{"name":"Regional API Gateway","responsibility":"Entry point for all customer and courier traffic in a region. Enforces authentication, rate limiting, and routes requests to regional microservices. Geo-DNS directs users to their home region's gateway.","boundaries":"Accepts only traffic routed via Geo-DNS or explicit region pinning. Rejects requests that don't match the region's customer registry."},{"name":"Regional Courier Assignment Service","responsibility":"Matches available couriers to deliveries within the region. Enforces the invariant that couriers can only be assigned to deliveries in their operating region.","boundaries":"Reads only from the regional courier registry and regional order queue. No cross-region courier data is accessible."},{"name":"Regional Event Bus","responsibility":"Async event backbone within a region. Publishes domain events (order created, delivery completed, courier assigned) for intra-region consumers. Also publishes sanitized, aggregated metric events to the Global Metrics Collector.","boundaries":"Raw events with PII stay within the region. Only pre-aggregated, anonymized metric payloads are emitted to the global tier."},{"name":"Global Control Plane","responsibility":"Lightweight coordination layer. Manages region registry, global configuration (feature flags, routing rules), and the global operations dashboard data pipeline. Does NOT store or process customer PII.","boundaries":"Receives only aggregated metrics (counts, latencies, throughput, error rates). Never receives customer names, addresses, or courier PII. Deployed in a neutral region or multi-region with no PII sensitivity."},{"name":"Global Metrics Aggregator","responsibility":"Collects pre-aggregated metric streams from each regional event bus. Stores time-series data for the global operations dashboard. Handles regional availability detection.","boundaries":"Ingests only anonymized operational metrics. If a region stops sending metrics, the aggregator marks that region as degraded in the dashboard."},{"name":"Global Operations Dashboard","responsibility":"Real-time view of all-region operational health: delivery volumes, SLA compliance, courier utilization, error rates. Consumed by global ops team.","boundaries":"Read-only view over the Global Metrics Aggregator. Cannot drill into individual customer or courier records (those require region-specific admin access)."},{"name":"Regional Degraded-Mode Service","responsibility":"Serves cached, read-only responses when the primary regional services are unhealthy. Allows customers to view recent order status and estimated delivery times from a read replica or local cache.","boundaries":"Activated only during regional primary failure. Serves stale-but-safe data from within the same region. No writes accepted in degraded mode except queuing retry requests."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Geo-DNS","to":"Regional API Gateway","contract":"DNS resolution returns the closest healthy regional gateway IP. Health checks every 10s. Failover within-region to secondary AZ, never cross-region for data-bearing traffic.","coupling_type":"infrastructure"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Regional Courier Assignment Service","contract":"gRPC/REST: POST /assignments {orderId, regionId} → {assignmentId, courierId}. Region ID is implicit from the gateway's deployment context.","coupling_type":"runtime-sync"},{"from":"Regional Event Bus","to":"Global Metrics Aggregator","contract":"Outbound metric events: {regionId, metricType, value, timestamp, window}. Schema is a Published Language. No PII fields. Push-based with at-least-once delivery.","coupling_type":"async-data"},{"from":"Global Metrics Aggregator","to":"Global Operations Dashboard","contract":"WebSocket/SSE subscription for real-time metric streams. REST for historical queries. All data is pre-aggregated by region.","coupling_type":"runtime-sync"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Regional Degraded-Mode Service","contract":"Circuit breaker pattern: if primary services fail health checks for >5s, gateway routes read requests to degraded-mode service. Write requests return 503 with retry-after header.","coupling_type":"runtime-sync-fallback"},{"from":"Global Control Plane","to":"Regional Data Plane (EU / US / APAC)","contract":"Configuration sync: feature flags, routing rules, schema versions pushed via config replication. Regional planes pull on startup and subscribe to change events. No PII in config payloads.","coupling_type":"async-config"}],"data_flow":"Customer request → Geo-DNS resolves to home region → Regional API Gateway authenticates and routes → Regional services process (order, assignment, tracking) using regional DB → Domain events published to Regional Event Bus → Regional consumers update read models, trigger notifications → Metric events (anonymized, aggregated) emitted from Regional Event Bus to Global Metrics Aggregator → Dashboard renders real-time cross-region view. On regional failure: gateway circuit breaker activates → degraded-mode service responds from cache/read-replica → metric stream to global aggregator stops → dashboard shows region as degraded.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Data Sovereignty via Regional Isolation","rationale":"GDPR and CCPA compliance require PII to never leave its jurisdiction. Rather than encrypting-at-rest with regional keys (which still risks transit violations), we enforce sovereignty architecturally: each region is a complete, independent deployment. This makes compliance verifiable by infrastructure topology, not just policy."},{"pattern":"Strangler Fig for Migration","rationale":"Existing EU-only platform can be progressively decomposed. New regions deploy the same artifact stack. EU remains primary until US and APAC are validated. Shared global components are extracted incrementally."},{"pattern":"CQRS for Global Dashboard","rationale":"The global dashboard is a read-only projection of regional write models. Separating the read path (aggregated metrics) from write path (regional operations) allows the dashboard to function independently of any single region's health."},{"pattern":"Circuit Breaker with Graceful Degradation","rationale":"Regional failures should not cascade. Circuit breaker at the API gateway level detects backend failures and routes to a degraded-mode service that serves cached data, meeting the requirement for degraded-but-functional service."},{"pattern":"Event-Carried State Transfer (Metrics Only)","rationale":"Regional event buses publish anonymized metric summaries to the global tier. This avoids synchronous cross-region calls (latency, coupling) and ensures PII never transits to the global layer."},{"pattern":"Geo-DNS with Health Checks","rationale":"Sub-100ms latency requires serving customers from infrastructure physically close to them. Geo-DNS routes to the nearest healthy region. No cross-region hops for customer-facing traffic."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order","OrderLine","DeliveryWindow"]},{"name":"Courier Operations","classification":"core","aggregates":["Courier","CourierAvailability","Assignment"]},{"name":"Customer Identity","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Customer","CustomerAddress","DataResidencyRecord"]},{"name":"Delivery Tracking","classification":"core","aggregates":["Delivery","TrackingEvent","ProofOfDelivery"]},{"name":"Global Operations Intelligence","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["RegionHealthMetric","AggregatedKPI","AlertRule"]},{"name":"Billing & Invoicing","classification":"generic","aggregates":["Invoice","PaymentRecord"]},{"name":"Region Configuration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["RegionDefinition","FeatureFlag","ComplianceRule"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Courier Operations","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order Management publishes OrderCreated events; Courier Operations consumes them to trigger assignment. Order Management dictates the delivery requirements contract."},{"upstream":"Courier Operations","downstream":"Delivery Tracking","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Assignment decisions flow downstream as AssignmentConfirmed events that Delivery Tracking consumes to initialize tracking."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Global Operations Intelligence","relationship":"Published Language. Order Management emits anonymized metric events conforming to a shared metrics schema. Global Ops consumes without influencing the upstream model."},{"upstream":"Courier Operations","downstream":"Global Operations Intelligence","relationship":"Published Language. Same pattern — anonymized courier utilization metrics flow to global aggregation."},{"upstream":"Region Configuration","downstream":"All Regional Contexts","relationship":"Conformist. Regional services conform to the configuration schema defined by the global control plane. Minimal coupling — only feature flags and routing rules."},{"upstream":"Customer Identity","downstream":"Order Management","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer. Order Management maintains its own lightweight customer reference (customerId + region) and does not import the full identity model. ACL translates at the boundary."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Fully independent regional deployments (no shared database, no cross-region replication of PII)","gains":"Iron-clad data residency compliance. Each region can be audited independently. No risk of accidental PII leakage via replication lag or misconfigured sync. Regulatory posture is provable by architecture, not policy.","sacrifices":"Higher infrastructure cost (3x the database, compute, and operational tooling). No global customer view — a customer who moves regions requires explicit data portability process. Deployment complexity increases (must deploy to 3 regions).","alternatives_considered":["Single global database with row-level security and regional encryption keys — rejected because transit of encrypted PII across regions may still violate GDPR data residency requirements and is harder to audit","Cross-region replication with PII filtering — rejected because filter misconfiguration is a compliance catastrophe"]},{"decision":"Aggregated metrics only in the global tier (no raw events)","gains":"Eliminates any PII exposure risk in the global layer. Simpler compliance story. Global tier can be hosted anywhere.","sacrifices":"Global ops team cannot drill into individual orders or customers from the dashboard — must use region-specific admin tools. Troubleshooting cross-region issues requires switching between regional admin interfaces.","alternatives_considered":["Tokenized PII in global tier — adds complexity and still creates a linkability risk","Full event replication with PII redaction — redaction errors are a compliance risk"]},{"decision":"Degraded mode via regional cache/read-replica rather than cross-region failover","gains":"Preserves data residency during outages. No risk of EU customer traffic being served from US infrastructure. Simpler failover logic.","sacrifices":"Degraded mode is truly degraded — no writes, stale data. Customers cannot place new orders during a regional outage. Recovery depends on regional infrastructure restoration.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region active-active with conflict resolution — violates data residency requirements","Multi-region active-active within the same jurisdiction (e.g., EU-West + EU-Central) — viable and recommended as a phase 2 enhancement, but adds complexity"]},{"decision":"Courier region-locking enforced at the domain model level (not just routing)","gains":"The invariant that a courier cannot be assigned cross-region is enforced in the Courier aggregate itself, not just at the API layer. Defense in depth.","sacrifices":"Couriers operating near borders (e.g., EU/APAC boundary) cannot be dynamically reassigned. Requires explicit region transfer process.","alternatives_considered":["Soft region preference with override capability — rejected because it weakens the invariant and creates compliance edge cases"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions","evolution_path":"The architecture is a template: deploy the same artifact stack to a new region, register it in the Global Control Plane, configure Geo-DNS. The regional data plane is self-contained by design, making region addition a deployment exercise rather than an architectural change."},{"area":"Intra-jurisdiction multi-AZ active-active","evolution_path":"Phase 2 can add active-active within a jurisdiction (e.g., EU-West-1 + EU-Central-1) for true HA without violating data residency. The regional event bus already supports this — just add a second consumer group in the secondary AZ."},{"area":"Global product catalog or pricing","evolution_path":"Non-PII data like product catalogs, pricing rules, and service definitions can be replicated globally via the Global Control Plane without compliance risk. This is a natural extension of the config sync interface."},{"area":"Regional microservice evolution","evolution_path":"Each region's internal architecture can evolve independently. One region could adopt event sourcing while another stays with traditional CRUD, as long as the external contracts (API, metric events) remain stable."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Data residency boundary","reason":"This is the core compliance invariant. PII must never cross regional boundaries. This constraint is non-negotiable and should be enforced at infrastructure (network policy), platform (no cross-region DB access), and application (domain model) levels. Changing this requires legal review."},{"area":"Metric event schema (Published Language)","reason":"The contract between regional event buses and the Global Metrics Aggregator must be stable. Breaking changes require coordinated rollout across all regions. Version the schema from day one."},{"area":"Region assignment on customer and courier entities","reason":"Once a customer or courier is assigned to a region, their data lives there. Migration requires an explicit, auditable data portability process. This rigidity is intentional — it prevents accidental data residency violations."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Clock skew and metric ordering across regions creates inconsistent global dashboard views","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use logical timestamps (vector clocks or Lamport timestamps) in metric events. Dashboard should display per-region timestamps and tolerate ordering gaps. Do not attempt global total ordering — it's unnecessary for operational metrics."},{"risk":"Regional deployment drift — regions running different versions causes behavioral inconsistency","severity":"high","mitigation":"Enforce same-version deployment across regions using a global CI/CD pipeline with canary releases. Region Configuration context tracks deployed versions. Automated drift detection alerts."},{"risk":"Degraded mode cache becomes stale beyond usefulness during extended outage","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Set explicit TTLs on cached data (e.g., 30 min for order status, 5 min for courier location). Display staleness indicators in the UI. After TTL expiration, show 'service unavailable' rather than misleading stale data."},{"risk":"Latency budget blown by regional service chain depth","severity":"high","mitigation":"Target <100ms requires careful service decomposition within each region. Limit synchronous call chains to 2-3 hops max. Use async processing for non-latency-critical paths. Mandate latency budgets per service (e.g., gateway: 5ms, auth: 10ms, core logic: 50ms, DB: 30ms, margin: 5ms)."},{"risk":"Compliance violation via logging or observability pipeline accidentally shipping PII to global tier","severity":"critical","mitigation":"All log and metric pipelines from regional to global must pass through a PII scrubbing gateway. Automated PII detection scanning on global-tier storage. Regular compliance audits. Infrastructure-level network policies blocking raw log export from regional VPCs."},{"risk":"Geo-DNS misconfiguration routes customers to wrong region","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Defense in depth: Regional API Gateway validates that the authenticated customer's home region matches the gateway's region. If mismatch, return a redirect to the correct region with no data processing. Automated Geo-DNS configuration tests in CI."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout: (1) Extract the existing EU monolith into the regional data plane template — define clear service boundaries, event contracts, and metric emission points. (2) Deploy the Global Control Plane and Metrics Aggregator alongside the existing EU deployment. (3) Deploy US region using the same template, validate with synthetic traffic. (4) Deploy APAC. (5) Enable Geo-DNS routing. (6) Harden degraded-mode and circuit breaker behavior. Each phase should be independently shippable and reversible.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/terraform/modules/regional-data-plane/ — Terraform module for region deployment (VPC, DB, compute, event bus)","infra/terraform/modules/global-control-plane/ — Global metrics aggregator, config store, dashboard infra","services/api-gateway/middleware/region-validator.ts — Validates customer region matches gateway region","services/courier-assignment/domain/courier-aggregate.ts — Enforces region-lock invariant on Courier aggregate","services/shared/events/metric-event-schema.ts — Published Language schema for cross-region metrics","services/api-gateway/middleware/circuit-breaker.ts — Circuit breaker with fallback to degraded-mode service","services/degraded-mode/cache-service.ts — Serves cached read-only responses during outages","infra/geo-dns/health-check-config.yaml — Geo-DNS routing and health check configuration","docs/adr/001-multi-region-data-sovereignty.md — Architecture Decision Record for data residency approach","docs/adr/002-global-metrics-aggregation.md — ADR for metrics-only global tier decision"],"patterns_to_follow":"Each regional service should be a self-contained bounded context deployment. Use domain events for intra-region communication. Use anonymized metric events for cross-region communication. Enforce data residency at three levels: infrastructure (network policies), platform (no cross-region DB connections), application (domain model invariants). Circuit breaker at gateway level. Published Language for all cross-region contracts.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do not introduce a global user directory or customer lookup service — this violates data residency","Do not use cross-region
